page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B processor operating at 2.9 GHz, configured with 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs with a single thread per core. The memory configuration consists of 8.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results show a divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance achieves average performance in single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU (1883.99) and Geekbench single-core (1556.0). However, multi-core and memory-bound tasks show limitations. Multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng (3871.28) and Geekbench (3343.0) is categorized as weak, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression is poor (21.05 GB/sec). Memory bandwidth is also weak, with large block read and write speeds measured at 30.35 GB/sec and 25.29 GB/sec respectively.

Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and the lack of local storage, this instance is designed for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or high-speed local disk I/O. It demonstrates average performance in static web serving throughput (6.33 GB/sec) and small-model LLM inference (107.65 tokens/sec for text generation with a 135M model). The dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable single-threaded execution, but the weak multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth limit its suitability for heavy database operations, large-scale software compilation, or memory-intensive data processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1556.0 and average static web serving throughput of 6.33 GB/sec. However, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are weak, as evidenced by a multi-core Geekbench score of 3343.0 and uncached read bandwidth of 30.35 GB/sec. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources with low memory requirements, such as lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing, and small-scale model inference, while being less suited for memory-intensive databases or parallelized compilation tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, showing average single-core but weak multi-core performance.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and a balanced resource profile for single-threaded and lightweight web workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B vCPUs at 2.9 GHz
8.0 GB of RAM with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and no local storage
Average single-core performance but weak multi-core scaling
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded tasks
